brief_justification: Exact correct answer (Month 6 / A) with valid arithmetic at every step; goes one level deeper by deriving the equilibrium threshold (~18,750) to explain why decline is inevitable, but stops there without exploring second-order dynamics; proceeds linearly with no named assumptions, biases, or mid-course reflection; the full table constitutes an implicit row-by-row recheck, and the equilibrium sanity-check adds a second angle, though no counterfactual or boundary test is run; no uncertainty acknowledged and no caveats about rounding or model assumptions; only one interpretation of the problem is considered and no alternatives (e.g., different ordering of growth vs. catch) are raised; every intermediate value is shown explicitly, making the full chain reproducible by a third party.
